summary: Exact accepted-ride totals require accounting for the ride dates and acceptance metrics; the twelve-month spine and ten fixed windows add only constant work, matching the linear worst-case input lower bound.

optimality.argument: In the worst case every ride is accepted. Changing any Rides row can move a distance and duration contribution between months, and changing any AcceptedRides row can alter both window totals. Exact results therefore require Omega(r+a) input work. One join/group scan plus constant-size calendar and window relations meets O(r+a).

replacement_checks.evidence: When every request is accepted, any one of the r request dates or a acceptance metric rows can alter at least one required window, establishing a linear worst-case lower bound.
The query scans and groups the ride/acceptance join once, then combines twelve monthly totals into ten fixed windows in O(r+a) total work.

Regression compares every authored fixture with an independent calendar oracle covering overlapping windows, empty months, date boundaries, monthly summation, rounding, rejected requests, out-of-year rides, and the December endpoint.
